name: Sklearn Gradient Boosting Regression
description: Gradient Boosting Regression builds a strong predictive model by combining multiple weak regression trees in a forward stage-wise manner. Each successive tree is trained to correct the errors made by the previous ensemble by minimizing a specified differentiable loss function.

fields.name: targetCol
fields.title: Target Column
fields.description: Target column containing the continuous outcome variable that the regression model learns to predict.

fields.name: featureCols
fields.title: Feature Columns
fields.description: List of input feature columns used for training. All features must be numeric, boolean, or vector-compatible.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Ratio used to split the dataset into training and testing sets. A value of 1.0 uses the full dataset for training.

fields.name: loss
fields.title: Loss
fields.description: Loss function optimized during training. Absolute error minimizes median error, squared error minimizes mean squared error, huber balances robustness and sensitivity, and quantile estimates conditional quantiles.

fields.name: learning_rate
fields.title: Learning Rate
fields.description: Controls how much each tree contributes to the overall model. Smaller values improve generalization but require more trees.

fields.name: n_estimators
fields.title: Number of Estimators
fields.description: Total number of regression trees added sequentially to build the gradient boosting ensemble.

fields.name: subsample
fields.title: Subsample
fields.description: Fraction of training samples used to fit each tree. Values below 1.0 introduce randomness and help reduce overfitting.

fields.name: criterion
fields.title: Criterion
fields.description: Metric used to evaluate split quality in trees. Friedman MSE is optimized specifically for gradient boosting performance.

fields.name: min_samples_split
fields.title: Min Samples Split
fields.description: Minimum number of samples required to split an internal node. Higher values result in simpler and more robust trees.

fields.name: min_samples_leaf
fields.title: Min Samples Leaf
fields.description: Minimum number of samples required at a leaf node to ensure stable predictions and reduce noise sensitivity.

fields.name: min_weight_fraction_leaf
fields.title: Min Weight Fraction Leaf
fields.description: Minimum weighted fraction of total sample weight required at a leaf node, useful when sample weights are applied.

fields.name: max_depth
fields.title: Max Depth
fields.description: Maximum depth of individual regression trees. Limits model complexity and controls overfitting.

fields.name: min_impurity_decrease
fields.title: Min Impurity Decrease
fields.description: Minimum reduction in impurity required to allow a node split. Larger values make the model more conservative.

fields.name: random_state
fields.title: Random State
fields.description: Seed value controlling randomness in subsampling and tree construction to ensure reproducible results.

fields.name: alpha
fields.title: Alpha
fields.description: Quantile value used for quantile regression and as the transition point between squared and absolute loss in Huber loss.

fields.name: verbose
fields.title: Verbose
fields.description: Controls the amount of logging output during training. Higher values provide more detailed progress information.

fields.name: max_leaf_nodes
fields.title: Max Leaf Nodes
fields.description: Maximum number of leaf nodes per tree. Restricts tree growth to manage model size and complexity.

fields.name: warm_start
fields.title: Warm Start
fields.description: Allows incremental training by reusing previously trained trees and adding new ones to the ensemble.

fields.name: presort
fields.title: Presort
fields.description: Indicates whether input data should be presorted to speed up split finding. Deprecated in newer sklearn versions.

fields.name: validation_fraction
fields.title: Validation Fraction
fields.description: Fraction of training data held out as a validation set for monitoring performance during early stopping.

fields.name: n_iter_no_change
fields.title: N Iter No Change
fields.description: Triggers early stopping if validation performance does not improve for the specified number of iterations.

fields.name: tol
fields.title: Tolerance
fields.description: Minimum improvement threshold required to continue training when early stopping is enabled.
